item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The former government's legislation suggest that the original inhabitants were incapable of dying,

I think that after becoming incapable of dying, the Foundation should test the usefulness of this object.

Anyway, that would be an interesting story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I get the impression that the person who wrote this REALLY hates religion.
Regardless I like it, pitch haven is one of my favorite canons on this site

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like one of the goals of this piece was to be atmospheric, and you succeeded in spades. 👍

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: I'm fine with the first one, but the others I'll have to reject. SCP-2999-A is not Sari, and SCP-1903 has no connotations to Freddie's snout removal.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: author partially approved edits

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

This is pretty much the center of the Pitch Haven universe. Judging from the author's post here, a lot of other anomalies are linked to this. I propose linking to them.

Editing this:

former head-crafters Fredrick and Agathos have forsaken

To this:

former head-crafters Fredrick and Agathos have forsaken

Editing this:

Crafters Suwaird and Sari will carry on their duties until our former leaders

To this:

Crafters **Suwaird** and **Sari** will carry on their duties until our former leaders

Editing this:

Fredrick's snout is to be removed from the remainder of his face

To this:

Fredrick's snout is to be removed from the remainder of his face

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It seems nobody else has talked about this code for some reason? But I cracked it pretty quickly (Just used a ton of Ctrl+F at first, then started compiling a few hints relevant to the last part.), but it was super engaging and fun. I think it basically worked how the author intended it. Didn't know what the answer meant until the very end, when I suddenly had an idea, and looked for that specific …uhh, thing.

Should I say what the answer is in a block, or just leave it up for people to solve it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly I probably would've sided with Freddie and pals.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's complicated. I probably should update my chart at some point because new information has come out since then..

Stuart in SCP-2999 is the reincarnation of the Suiward in this article, the entity pretending to be be Sarah is Clovis from this article. Both Stuart and Sarah were infected with SCP-1903.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry if I'm doing this wrong, I admit this is my first time commenting in a discussion, and I'm doing this from mobile on top of that.

Anyway, since I guess I'm being a bit slow to the draw here, would somebody explain how 1903 and 2999 relate to this skip? Outside the animal themes of course.

I also apologize for posting on a discussion so old, promise not to make a habit of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry for my english. I've been confused when I saw the "functioned off of" (just don't understand what it's meaning). Thanks for the answer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No mistake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"funtioned?"

I don't think there's a mistake?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hello there! I'm just wonder about one word in the text…

SCP-2746-1's economy functioned off of a barter system

"funtioned off of". This is a mistake? Correct me if I'm wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is just a substitution cipher and not a real conlang (which is probably the only really disappointing thing about Pitch Haven, IMO). Someone posted a key, either in this thread or the hub (but you could figure it out easily from the "translated" documents here).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So the wierd numbers appear to be in hexadecimal code. :p feeling so smart now.
translated back they reveal. ..

Ioy �uay pi'ual suai'yi i'gua yi�zyua'w wiri'fo.
. now someone with good translation skills.. please help me

item.title: The cipher.
item.content:encoded:
If you really need to know how the First Language works…

+ Spoilers!
- click to hide Ythill forever

It's a (relatively) straight cipher from English. To translate into First Language, take the English letters and transform them like this:

A = Æ
B = P
C = A
D = K
E = Y
F = S
G = J
H = O
I = LA
J = U
K = Z
L = IR
M = YI
N = L
O = I'
P = V
Q = KH
R = UA
S = W
T = F
U = G
V = PH
W = IO
X = DE'
Y = IL
Z = XU

Yes, sometimes one letter maps to two letters, and those two letters might be used separately elsewhere. Use your best judgment when backtranslating.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
• This is the First Language, and therefore probably comparable to all human language which ultimately descend from it.
  • This is God's language and therefore is the language of the Word, and therefore the language that the universe is written in.
  • Some people are incarnate Edenites, and were created fluent in it, whether they remember that or not.
  • The Foundation had two of the most significant Crafters as employees. One of whom was a polyglot genius and Site-45's subject matter expert on folklore.
  • If SCP-073 exists in Pitch Haven (no idea), it was his native language and he possesses perfect recall.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Linguists need something to work with besides foreign text itself. The text needs to be similar enough to a language that's already understood, or they need a Rosetta Stone, or something. Without that sort of context translating the text would require something anomalous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's what linguists go to school for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How did they translate the non-English stuff? Was there something like a Rosetta Stone somewhere?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's weird number sequences, and the author doesn't know where they came from.

Welp, it looks like we're living in a middling-quality creepypasta. It was nice knowing you all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

What's with this code?

49 6f 79 20 e6 75 61 79 20 70 69 27 75 61 6c 20 73 75 61 69 27 79 69 20 69 27 67 75 61 20 79 69 e6 7a 79 75 61 27 77 20 77 69 72 69 27 66 6f 2e

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do like a good shaggy god story, but not one so shaggy as that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find it somewhat anticlimatic (I just am not too much of a fan of grand unifying theories or "everything happens for a reason"), but I must hand it to you for still making it into a very good narrative.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think my favourite part of this might be that the gateway to the Garden of Eden, paradise itself, is located just outside of Sin City. Beautiful. +1

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Okay, I guess it was a little rude. I'll try to be less sarcastic in the future. :)

item.title: Re: Pitch Haven Grimdark?
item.content:encoded:
I think if you make the solution to theogony an evil, petty, but completely omnipotent God, you are pretty much in Chaos Gods territory.

In the grim (pitch) dark haven of the Garden of Eden there was only war…

item.title: Re: Pitch Haven Grimdark?
item.content:encoded: Oh, it is. But I was mainly going for a horror/tragedy/fantasy in doing so (… Is that considered grimdark?)

item.title: Pitch Haven Grimdark?
item.content:encoded:
Fair enough. I may not be attempting it to be grimdark.

You've hinted at this before. Maybe I am totally getting it wrong but isn't Pitch Haven a maltheistic cosmology that inverts Gnostic theogony? It is a world where goodness is invariably punished. Where suffering is repeated again and again across multiple incarnations like an inescapable version of samsara. How is this not an unrelentingly horrific universe?

item.title: Re: SCP-2746-EX-J
item.content:encoded:
Fair enough. I may not be attempting it to be grimdark, but I do take pleasure in the fact that at least there's a good reason for it if it is. Glad you like it by the way! U^w^U

As for the kid part… Eh, seems like a stretch to me personally, but I could see it like that. However, in this case, they have referred to me as a fifteen year old, and then attempted to use "kid" as an ad hominem.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The way you phrased any critique made it seem less like pointing out stuff for the author to use to improve the article and more like writing a post to get off as much snark as possible. Many of your statements and attempts to critique are downright inflammatory and rude, and you definitely would be warned for those even if they weren't dressed up in a "joke SCP". Saying there's "no malicious intent" doesn't excuse the content of the post itself.

While Fantem addressed specific points in a non-official manner, I'm doing so in an official manner. Please take the time to read the Criticism Policy before giving more feedback. This is an official warning.

item.title: Re: SCP-2746-EX-J
item.content:encoded:
Well I am a huge fan, but I do find the weird inverse gnosticism in Pitch Haven to be among the most unrelentingly grimdark stuff on the site. On the other hand, I like it!

Also, I think of twenty year olds as kids, and I think so do a lot of people that are old enough to be your parents (including probably your parents). :)

item.title: Re: SCP-2746-EX-J
item.content:encoded:
Okay… that was kinda unnecessary. I would have preferred it if you just told me what was bothering you about it rather than writing up some passive-aggressive retcon about it, but okay. The way you've written it, I feel like I'm missing a large chunk of your critique, but addressing what points I can find:

[+] Show critique of... I don't know what this is
[-] Close critique of... something

Document #2746-EX-ORIGIN, according to the header on each page, has been printed out from the site fur████████.███ (uncensored, furaffinity.net), an online community frequented by adherents of the so-called "furry" subculture. (No, I'm not going to explain it for you. If you're interested, google it. But don't say I didn't warn you. — Dr. ███████)

Yes, there are furry influences in this. I am a part of this subculture (though, I frequent tumblr furry blogs way more than I have ever frequented FA. Don't even have a real account there). However, in terms of the series, I have reason for that.

It boils down to "why would they be humans at all?" If they were going to choose a form for themselves, especially one where humans were of little consequence at the time, and only had one designer, why would they be humans? It makes much more sense to model themselves after a species that they were particularly proud of, or at least one that they were fond of.

Also, can we please stop pointing at subcultures we aren't a part of and going "eww, im not gonna explain it, you google it," as if we're children avoiding someone we don't like because they have cooties? It's a fandom dedicated to anthropomorphic animals. And, if you want to get into the porn, because that definition is so broad, the fandom tends to mix with other fandoms and fetishes, and creates a lot of porn.

…displaying heavy influence of the style and worldview of the works of JRR Tolkien, especially the Silmarillion. (Including a tendency toward detailed world-building and the use of complex artificial languages.)

I… do?… Well, Tolkien isn't who I expected to be compared to. In terms of world-building, maybe, but I'd hardly use the creation of a language to mean I have the man's style… Admittedly though, I haven't actually read too much of Tolkien's work, so I can't really deny any similarities. I do deny any influence from his works however.

The focus of the novel is mostly on juvenile musings about morality, specifically whether God can, or even should, be measured by the same moral framework used for other beings, and whether faith and obedience can override general decency as the measure of true good.

That's your definition of juvenile? Most adults couldn't come to a consensus on that. And I'm not musing about that. I'm stating.

There is much allusion to interspecies sexuality…

They are the same species. They are polymorphic gods. They only look like animals. Form is irrelevant. Although intercorse and sexual relations are a thing socially, they produce asexually with the option of collaboration.

and gratuitious display of violence and cruelty, in an apparent attempt to make the novel seem ("quote-unquote") risque or mature.

It's hardly gratuitous, and there is purpose in it. I explained this further on, but to address the claim here, I didn't tack on unnecessary violence just to be edgy.

According to D-100182, a former high school English teacher sentenced to Death for [DATA EXPUNGED] (What has THAT got to do with SCP-2746?! — The Ethics Committee)…

Noting that I have had two college English professors give positive reviews on this, and that this sentence only serves to give the following statements false authority. And, let's say you actually were a high school English teacher, it'd still be kinda a dick move to include an argument from authority in what is meant to just serve as a critique.

Also, unrelated, if that was meant to be a joke at the end there, I don't get it.

it's "decent writing for a 15 year old"…

I'm twenty, nineteen at time of writing, and that was pretty rude, especially for an unfounded statement.

…but "fails to hold up either aesthetically, or as a piece of moral dialogue"…

It wasn't meant to be a piece of moral dialogue, and it appearing to not hold up aesthetically is either false (because it's clearly holding up), or your opinion. While yes, you are totally entitled to your personal opinion in… whatever this is, the later should not be said while you are claiming authority, especially without citing evidence.

… and is characterized by "horrid characterization"

First, the characters relevant to the plot have already been established elsewhere, and this does not serve to introduce them. This is why I recommended reading the articles in the order published, rather than in chronological order, at least on your first go-round. However, it is my belief that it still gets the job done, and well, since I write these with the intent for them to stand on their own, and be strengthened by their relatives.

Second… I'm sorry you feel that way? I can't really tell what you have a problem with. You'd have to tell me something that's wrong here specifically before I can believe your claim.

"blatant sockpuppetry"

Do you mean straw-manning? Because Sockpuppeting means I created another account to deceive people, and that'd be a very serious accusation if you were. If you mean straw-manning, please, tell me where I did, because I do not see how I misconstrued the fictional characters I created.

"depiction of cruelty so grimdark and pointless that it borders on self-parody".

Could you please give some examples? Merely being violent does not make something grimdark. And no, it did have a point. It had a huge point. Each one of those punishments were there for a reason. I explained the symbolism behind the punishments as I described them, and they are referenced throughout the series frequently.

And they're punishments, in ancient times, performed directly by gods… what did you expect? Of course they'd be cruel.

While it never has God appear in any of its scenes, it alludes to him having a physical form made of flesh and blood, and that form being humanoid.

No it doesn't? A humanoid form, yeah, but I never said anything about a physical form.

The novel was written in a tone highly critical and spiteful of humanity, with undertones of animal rights activism and naturalism.

… No it wasn't? When did I ever?

Much of the plot centers around the disaster the angels have to live through…

True (though, they technically aren't angels, so much as devils).

…after humanity's deliberate and malicious descent into sin.

False. Some of the Furies (as in, these Furies, not the 1913 Leaders of the Furies, plus golem) might have viewed it that way, but they blamed the serpent more for it than mankind. And humanity at the time sure as hell wasn't "deliberately and maliciously descending into sin" as you claim I'm making them being (and you wanna talk about straw-manning?).

████████ has been found on-line on fur████████.███, as one of the most highly regarded works posted on the site, with entire fan-sites devoted to it.

… Are you complaining that people enjoy this? If so, people are entitled to their opinion. Granted, just because something's popular, that doesn't serve as a sure sign of quality, but its mention here comes off as salt that people like something you don't.

However, a fan forum was found by MTF Gamma-5 ("Red Herrings"), dedicated to a semi-serious religious cult based around ████████. Its members profess to being "otherkin" (Google it, but beware! — Dr. ███████), and hold ████████ to be prophetic and factually true.

Dude… I talked about this earlier, and while I myself might not be otherkin, that's still just fucking rude, and bears repeating. At best, it's an inflammatory comment towards otherkin, and detached from the piece. At worst, it's accusing the fans of these pieces of being furries or otherkin. Either way, that's incredibly offensive.

According to our psychiatric records, SCP-2746 was responsible for more psychotic breakdowns than any other SCP we are containing.

No.

I'm glad it turned out to be a bad fanfic written by a kid, because that's what it sounded like all the time.

Again, twenty years old, and thanks for being condescending af.

TL/DR: Your parody(?) was passive aggressive af, and felt like a shallow attempt to get away with claims that would otherwise earn you side glances at best.

item.title: SCP-2746-EX-J
item.content:encoded:
Meh, far too cheesy for me… Allow me to voice my criticism in the form of a joke SCP. I do respect the awesome amount of work that went into this SCP, so please take it with a grain of salt. No malicious intent present. ;)

+ SCP-2746-EX-J
- SCP-2746-EX-J

SCP-2746-EX

To be circulated to all personnel involved in, ever having been involved in, or having knowledge of the research or containment of SCP-2746, and additionally to all mental health staff. The anomaly categorized as SCP-2746, following the events detailed in Document #2746-EX-I, has been re-categorized as SCP-2746-EX. All containment protocols are hereby rescinded, and research terminated.

The recovered item designated as SCP-826' is hereby assigned the Safe class. It is to be transferred to site ███, and surrendered into the custody of Dr. ██████, who is to begin testing on it to determine any differences in its properties to the SCP-826 already in his custody, with a focus on the differences between the known modus operandi of SCP-826 and the observed properties of SCP-2746-1. He is to be supplied with a copy of Document #2746-EX-I and of Document #2746-EX-ORIGIN.

The site previously designated as SCP-2746-1 should be screened for any remaining anomalous effects, and upon a negative conclusion, Site 21-A is to be decommissioned after due cleanup procedures.

"Glad we got this one out of the way kids, huh?" — O5-██

Document #2746-EX-I

During an exploration by Agents █████ and ██████ on ██/██/201█, focusing on the retrieval of documents of interest from SCP-2746-1, Agent ██████ has discovered in a library compound a sheaf of copy paper, apparently printed with a laser printer, measuring approximately 600 printed pages, and bound together with a pink plastic comb (to be further referenced as Document #2746-EX-ORIGIN), between a pair of pewter bookends shaped like unicorn heads (designated SCP-826', Safe class).

After removing Document #2746-EX-ORIGIN from the shelf and leaving the library, both agents found themselves immediately outside the entrance of SCP-2746-1 in human form. Attempting to return to SCP-2746-1, they did not experience any transfiguration, and found a cavernous, empty area within that has since been confirmed to be a decommissioned military ammunition storage. The cavern showed minor signs of having been inhabited by vagrants in the recent past, such as litter consisting of junk food packaging, used ███████ and ████████████, cigarette butts and traces of recreational drugs, cold fireplaces and piles of █████.

The agents have successfully recovered SCP-826' from a makeshift bookshelf containing mostly █████ magazines showing signs of heavy use.

Document #2746-EX-ORIGIN, according to the header on each page, has been printed out from the site fur████████.███, an online community frequented by adherents of the so-called "furry" subculture. (No, I'm not going to explain it for you. If you're interested, google it. But don't say I didn't warn you. — Dr. ███████)

It is an epic fantasy novel of approximately 600 printed pages in length, titled ████████, displaying heavy influence of the style and worldview of the works of JRR Tolkien, especially the Silmarillion. (Including a tendency toward detailed world-building and the use of complex artificial languages.) It re-tells the story of the creation, original sin, and apocalypse according to the Bible, but with several marked shifts in tone, viewpoint, and major changes to the events described in the Bible. It spans the entire lifetime of the created world, with most of the plot focusing on events from the Book of Genesis, and a smaller, but significant part on the Book of Revelations.

Consistent with what we know about SCP-826, Document #2746-EX-ORIGIN has detailed descriptions of all explorations by Foundation personnel, as well as several [DATA EXPUNGED].

The focus of the novel is mostly on juvenile musings about morality, specifically whether God can, or even should, be measured by the same moral framework used for other beings, and whether faith and obedience can override general decency as the measure of true good. There is much allusion to interspecies sexuality, and gratuitious display of violence and cruelty, in an apparent attempt to make the novel seem ("quote-unquote") risque or mature.

According to D-100182, a former high school English teacher sentenced to Death for [DATA EXPUNGED] (What has THAT got to do with SCP-2746?! — The Ethics Committee) who was tasked by Dr. ███████ to read, summarize and critique the novel, it's "decent writing for a 15 year old", but "fails to hold up either aesthetically, or as a piece of moral dialogue", and is characterized by "horrid characterization", "blatant sockpuppetry" and "depiction of cruelty so grimdark and pointless that it borders on self-parody".

████████ portrays the angels of God as talking animals, and describes the kingdom of plants and animals to have been created by them, as opposed to by God himself, who has only created the angels themselves, and humans. While it never has God appear in any of its scenes, it alludes to him having a physical form made of flesh and blood, and that form being humanoid. The novel was written in a tone highly critical and spiteful of humanity, with undertones of animal rights activism and naturalism. Much of the plot centers around the disaster the angels have to live through after humanity's deliberate and malicious descent into sin.

████████ has been found on-line on fur████████.███, as one of the most highly regarded works posted on the site, with entire fan-sites devoted to it. The original, on-line copy doesn't contain any references to Foundation incursions or [DATA EXPUNGED], as expected. Both ████████ and fur████████.███ have been determined to be completely non-anomalous, and posing no risk whatsoever. However, a fan forum was found by MTF Gamma-5 ("Red Herrings"), dedicated to a semi-serious religious cult based around ████████. Its members profess to being "otherkin" (Google it, but beware! — Dr. ███████), and hold ████████ to be prophetic and factually true. An old topic on this forum contains several references to the location of SCP-2746-EX. Undercover investigation of the cult is ongoing.

Remarks by Dr. ███████████ of the mental health task force

As all of you above Security Level 3 are aware, we are containing far too many anomalies of a religious nature, most of which imply vastly diverging, and often mutually exclusive cosmologies.

I have seen good operatives descend into depression and psychosis from religious distress on such grounds. According to our psychiatric records, SCP-2746 was responsible for more psychotic breakdowns than any other SCP we are containing. I myself had trouble coming to terms with some of its revelations. I'm glad it turned out to be a bad fanfic written by a kid, because that's what it sounded like all the time.

This information will save the minds of many a good agent. Thanks, everyone. Wish us more such luck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The last document reminds me so vividly of the fantastic insane drama that I watched go down on a forum I used to love. It really mirrors how I feel about that place now.

item.title: Re: Discrepancy?
item.content:encoded: Well, technically Fredrick made them in the maker's image, but you're right.

item.title: Re: And now, in weirdo animal language...
item.content:encoded: Sweet fucking crap… you deserve some kind of medal for that.

item.title: Re: Discrepancy?
item.content:encoded:
Well there were also two or maybe three humans created directly by the maker, right? In his image and all that. But these weren't archon spirits probably but actual human type people. Most of the article is talking about what happened in Eden in the millenia after the fall from grace (event Nachash).

Although I am still very confused by the Maker in particular so I might be totally wrong here.

item.title: Discrepancy?
item.content:encoded:
Records located within SCP-2746-1 suggest that the landmass was inhabited by sapient, non-
human animals, estimated 770 to 780 in number.

So how is the following possible?

According to recovered documents, Event Nachash was the result of 'the Maker' declaring that
humans were to be banished from SCP-2746-1, and that a penalty requiring that all citizens
acquire the need to eat in order to 'preserve their sanity'.

I made the text bold, of course. I might be missing something.

item.title: And now, in weirdo animal language...
item.content:encoded: I said I'd do it and I fuckin' did it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a human travels through SCP-2746, they will take on the physical appearance of a non-human animal;

Super upvote /me wants to be a dragon c:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To tell you the truth, I'd be using hieroglyphics if I could… I just can't find text for it…

Wait, it probably exists as a font, doesn't it? Please tell me it does. I don't know how to work fonts on this site, but if someone could help me out with that, I would love them forever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One thing that kinda bothers me is the english letters in the old angelic language, but that is a very minor gripe and im sure that it has good reason for being like that. Other than that this is excellent and +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, considering that this is about talking animals and Christianity, does it connect to SCP-1845, in a sort of progression? Like the remnants of the inhabitants of scp-2746, migrate and become 1619, or something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When you think about it, this whole Eden thing was bound to get ugly. The Maker basically said "Creation isn't going to make itself. I could do it but there is a Murder, She Wrote marathon on tonight.. I got it! I'll make a bunch of servants to run the place for Me! I'll give them intelligence, free will, and hopes and dreams, and then keep them as slaves/unpaid interns for eternity! It'll be great!" Then He micromanaged all the work He was too lazy to do himself and only approved after inserting His Author Avatar Mary Sue custom race in the mix. When that inevitably blew up in everyone's faces (snouts?) because the setting totally wasn't designed for a munchkin race like humanity, He went full tilt diva on the Angels. "Am I out of touch?" the Maker wondered. "No, it's the Angels who are wrong." "This sucks," said some of the Angels, after getting demoted and watching Humanity getting promoted to a better department. "We should kill that asshole and take His stuff!" And the whole deal just ended in tears.

Moral of the story? Don't shove in your Original Content (Don't steal!) totally awesome custom race into a established setting while shitting all over the other races. It always breeds resentment in the party. Also, if you just have to create a slave race to lord over then make sure they are dumb. Otherwise you are just asking for trouble.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy crap, +1 from me, if only for the correct usage of 'sapience' instead of 'sentience'.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded:
AAAAAAAAaaaaaw yeeeah!

Seriously, I never thought I'd get to this point in life.

I am proud of this series.
Great job, Fantem! (^_^)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+For Fantem Eyes Only
-Hide teh spoilers
  1. For some reason I thought SCP-1913-2 was a monkey. It's not. It's a dog person.
  2. I can see all of that, yeah.
  3. I counted Crowley's death as a shenanigan. I am an awful person.
  4. I want to give Stuart a hug.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
[+] The answers
[-] Teh troof is 2 much
  • Yes
  • yes with the caveat that the angels did far more than help
  • yes
  • yes
  • yes (monkey?)
  • yes
  • there are several steps missing between here and there and I wouldn't call Jackie insane, but rather than soulless
  • that happened before Clovis turned into Jackie
  • yes
  • missed Sarah's death but yes
  • very yes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ohhhh I like this :D o.o Can the SCP make a Base here for the poor little animals?
Also their "MAKER" seems like a…. um… *Looks through book of bad words to properly describe*…. a real A-hole. Since bad isn't enough to describe them."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ The Full Story, maybe?
  • God creates Eden.
  • God creates angels to help him with designing shit.
  • Angels create animals, take on their creations' respective forms.
  • God makes humans, humans fuck everything up.
  • Freddie Wolf and family start rebellion (the monkey isn't important)
  • Freddie Wolf and family are punished.
  • Somehow, Freddie's co-conspirator is turned into an insane human prostitute with rabbit ears and a weird memetic thing going on.
  • The leaders of Heaven decide to off themselves and become humans, on Earth.
  • Stuart Hayward and Sarah Crowley's shenanigans.
  • Whatever the hell happened to kill and ressurect Hayward in 2999.
  • More things that have a creepy aesthetic but aren't actually all that creepy when you think about it, but are definitely tragic, because Stuart Hayward's misery never ends!!
-Y/N?

item.title: Re: Disgusted?
item.content:encoded:
Eh, you aren't entirely off. I took a lot of things into consideration when writing the series, like what GOIs would probably be affected, skips that were similar, and could possibly be related, that sort of thing.

Though, no. That's not 682.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Are Suwaird and Sari meant to be
- Steward (Hayward) and Sarah (Crowely)?

The two of them left Eden and then became the mortal humans they wanted to become, maybe just with no memories of Eden or their past selves? Then they meet up again and become entwined through some cosmic fate.


Also, 2746 is located near Las Vegas, and Site-45-C is located in or near Las Vegas. I'm gonna assume this is also purposeful.

Looking forward to you finished up your skips and continuing to flesh out your story.

item.title: Re: Disgusted?
item.content:encoded: True, but he leaps to mind, in that this maker finds presumably benign things disgusting and is unspeakably cruel.

item.title: Re: Disgusted?
item.content:encoded: 682 isn't the only one who can experience disgust, you know.

item.title: Disgusted?
item.content:encoded:
We presented our work to the Maker, like a child does with its first work of art, and he looked upon it all.

It disgusted him.

Is this…682?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I saw the name, my first thought was "did they make 'Paul is dead' into an SCP".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This and the related skips just blow me away, even though I still don't fully understand what's going on. Take my upvote.
